story_clusters.summary: West Bengal is holding its assembly elections in multiple phases, with over 3.6 crore voters eligible for the first phase on April 23, according to the Election Commission. The polls have seen political tensions, including allegations by West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee of attempts to cancel her nomination in Bhowanipore and debates over the Uniform Civil Code becoming a campaign issue between the TMC and BJP. Additionally, the Supreme Court is set to hear a plea against the Election Commission freezing electoral rolls ahead of the elections.

perspectives.unique_angle: One-hour pause, tighter checks: RBI’s plan to tackle rising digital payment fraud One-hour pause, tighter checks: RBI’s plan to tackle rising digital payment fraud

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has issued a Discussion Paper proposing safeguards to curb digital payment frauds, including potential 1-hour delays and customer controls. The paper, titled Digital Payment Fraud Safeguards, is open for public comments and aims to enhance security in cross-border inward payments, as outlined in the Faster Cross initiative. The RBI also seeks feedback on measures for Timely Credit and Border Inward Payments to address rising fraud cases.

story_clusters.summary: The Government of India launched a two-year nationwide commemoration of Mahatma Jyotiba Phule’s 200th birth anniversary, running from April 11, 2026, to April 11, 2028. A national tribute was held at Parliament House on his bicentenary, marking a historic first, with a commemorative program also taking place at the Dr. Ambedkar International Center.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) conducted a ₹2-lakh crore Variable Rate Reverse Repo (VRRR) auction to absorb surplus liquidity, marking its first such move this year. This action led to an increase in bond yields and swap rates, reflecting tighter liquidity conditions in the market. Multiple reports confirm that the RBI's move aims to manage excess cash in the financial system.

story_clusters.summary: Delhi’s draft EV Policy 2.0 proposes a 100% road tax waiver for electric cars priced up to ₹30 lakh and a ban on new non-electric two-wheeler registrations. story_clusters.summary: India is resuming Qatari LPG and LNG imports via the Strait of Hormuz following a ceasefire, with an Indian LPG tanker crossing the strait as the first shipment since the agreement. Multiple reports confirm that the Hormuz deal is facilitating the resumption of these energy imports to India.

articles.title: BCI Withdraws 3-Year Moratorium on New Law Colleges | What It Means for Law Aspirants 2026
articles.summary: The Bar Council of India (BCI) has lifted a three-year moratorium on the establishment of new law colleges. This decision will allow aspiring law students to enroll in newly approved institutions starting from 2026. The move aims to address the growing demand for legal education while ensuring regulatory oversight. The BCI is the statutory body regulating legal education and practice in India.
articles.raw_summary: The post BCI Withdraws 3-Year Moratorium on New Law Colleges | What It Means for Law Aspirants 2026 appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: Centre notifies CAPF Act after President’s assent
articles.summary: The Central Government has notified the Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) Act following the President's assent. This move formalizes the legal framework governing the CAPFs. The notification is a procedural step that activates the law, impacting the functioning and authority of these security forces. The CAPF Act regulates the deployment, powers, and operational guidelines for forces like the BSF, CRPF, and CISF.

articles.title: Cabinet clears amendments to women reservation Bill
articles.summary: The Indian Cabinet has approved amendments to the Women Reservation Bill. The bill aims to reserve one-third of seats in the Lok Sabha and state legislatures for women. This move is part of efforts to enhance women's political representation in governance. The next steps involve parliamentary proceedings for the bill's passage.

articles.title: RBI cancels the licence of National Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Pratapgarh
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI), vide order dated April 10, 2026, has cancelled the licence of  National Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Pratapgarh , under Section 22 read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act). Consequently, the bank ceases to carry on banking business, with effect from the close of business on April 10, 2026. The Commissioner and Registrar of Cooperative, Uttar Pradesh has also been requested to issue an order for winding up the bank and appoint a liquidator

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that multi-state co-operative societies can only invest in companies that are their subsidiaries or operate in the same line of business, as per their bye-laws and Section 64 of the Multi-State Co-operative Societies Act, 2002. The ruling restricts investment flexibility for these societies.

articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant stated that courts should grant anti-arbitration injunctions only in exceptional circumstances to preserve the autonomy of arbitral tribunals. The CJI also emphasized that arbitral tribunals must retain control over the use of technology and AI tools in their proceedings. This ruling underscores the balance between judicial oversight and the independence of arbitration processes in India.

articles.title: Co-operative Society can only submit resolution plan for corporate debtor in same line of business
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a Multi-State Co-operative Society can only submit a resolution plan for a corporate debtor operating in the same line of business, as per Section 64(d) of the Multi-State Co-operative Societies Act, 2002. This means such societies are ineligible to propose resolution plans for corporate debtors in unrelated sectors. The decision impacts the participation of co-operative societies in the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) resolution process.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court held that section 64(d) of the Multi-State Co-operative Societies Act, 2002 restricts Multi-State Co-operative Society investment only to entities in the same line of business. Accordingly, Multi-State Co-operative Society is ineligible to submit resolution plan for corporate debtor which doesn’t operate in same line of business. articles.title: Duty Liability Upheld as Notification Subsequent to Bill of Entry Filing Cannot Apply Retrospectively
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that exemption benefits under a customs duty notification cannot be claimed if the notification is issued after the filing of Bills of Entry. The tribunal clarified that the duty liability is determined based on the date of filing the Bill of Entry, not the date of clearance. This decision impacts importers seeking retrospective duty exemptions and reinforces the principle that post-filing notifications cannot apply retroactively.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that exemption benefits cannot be claimed under a notification issued after the filing of Bills of Entry. It ruled that the relevant date for duty determination is the date of filing, not clearance. The post Duty Liability Upheld as Notification Subsequent to Bill of Entry Filing Cannot Apply Retrospectively appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Southern districts, once known as a laggard, seek to shed the bad image
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's Thoothukudi district has seen significant infrastructure and energy developments in recent months, including the establishment of an electric vehicle plant, the commissioning of a 660-MW thermal power plant, and the initiation of tendering for an outer harbour at the port. These projects aim to improve the region's industrial and energy capacity, addressing its past reputation as a laggard in development. articles.title: Service Tax Not Applicable on Freight Margins Due to Principal-to-Principal Transactions
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that margins earned from buying and selling cargo space are trading profits, not service taxable income, as these transactions are conducted on a principal-to-principal basis. The ruling clarifies that such freight margins are not liable for service tax. The decision impacts businesses involved in logistics and freight services by providing clarity on tax obligations for principal-agent transactions.

articles.title: At ICA conference, CJI Surya Kant underscores arbitration’s role in global economy
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant stated at an arbitration conference that the term 'Alternative Dispute Resolution' may need to be reconsidered, as arbitration is no longer an alternative but a primary method of resolving disputes. The CJI emphasized arbitration's growing importance in the global economy and its role in reducing litigation burdens. The remarks were made during a discussion on the evolving landscape of dispute resolution in India.
articles.raw_summary: Chief Justice Kant expressed that, “it may be time to rethink the very expression ‘Alternative Dispute Resolution’ since it is no longer an alternative to litigation”.

articles.summary: Haryana Police has removed over 6,000 links and apps as part of a campaign targeting online content that glorifies weapons, promotes violence, or displays obscene material involving women and children. The Director General of Police (DGP) announced strict action against such content, emphasizing the state's commitment to curbing harmful digital material. This move reflects broader efforts to regulate online platforms and protect vulnerable groups in India.

articles.title: Kerala Assembly elections 2026: LDF unveils lists of CPI(M) CPI and independent candidates
articles.summary: The Left Democratic Front (LDF) in Kerala announced candidate lists for the 2026 Assembly elections under the 'Nava Keralam' banner. The Communist Party of India (Marxist) declared 86 candidates, including incumbent Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan, while the Communist Party of India listed 25. Polling is scheduled for April 9, with vote counting on May 4. The election results could impact future state policies and governance in Kerala.
articles.raw_summary: The LDF announced candidates for Kerala Assembly elections under 'Nava Keralam'. CPI(M) declared 86, including CM Vijayan, and CPI listed 25. Polling is on April 9 and counting of votes on May 4.

articles.title: Unexplained Cash Deposits Addition Reduced Because Only Peak Balance Taxable: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad upheld the validity of reassessment based on cash deposit information but reduced the taxable addition by applying the 'peak credit theory' due to withdrawal patterns. This ruling clarifies that only peak balances in cash deposits are taxable, not the entire unexplained deposits. The decision affects how tax authorities assess unexplained cash transactions and could influence future tax policy interpretations. The case was reported by TaxGuru
articles.raw_summary: ITAT confirmed validity of reassessment based on cash deposit information. However, it reduced addition by applying peak credit theory due to withdrawal patterns The post Unexplained Cash Deposits Addition Reduced Because Only Peak Balance Taxable: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Sabarimala Day 3: Govt urges ‘desi lens’ for Articles 25-26, says SC orders relied on Western definition
articles.summary: A nine-judge Supreme Court bench led by CJI Surya Kant is hearing a reference on the interplay between Articles 25 and 26 of the Indian Constitution, which deal with religious freedom. The government has urged the court to adopt an 'Indian lens' for interpreting these articles, arguing that the Supreme Court's previous orders relied on Western definitions. The case has significant implications for religious practices and constitutional rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: The interplay between Articles 25 and 26 is central to the Sabarimala reference, which a nine-judge Supreme Court bench led by CJI Surya Kant is hearing.

articles.title: So, Here's the Problem: Textile Factories in 10 Nations Face Inconsistent Environmental Permit Rules, UN Study Finds
articles.summary: A UNEP report found that environmental permitting systems in ten major textile-producing nations, including India, have significant gaps in regulating industrial pollution from textile factories. Most national laws do not classify textile activities as environmentally significant or consider gendered impacts on workers. The study highlights inconsistencies in how spinning mills, dye houses, and garment factories are regulated, raising concerns about worker safety and environmental compliance.
articles.raw_summary: Environmental permitting systems across ten major textile-producing nations contain significant gaps in how industrial pollution from spinning mills, dye houses and garment factories is regulated, a new UNEP report has found. Most national legislation fails to classify textile activities as environmentally significant or to consider gendered impacts on workers.

articles.title: Service Tax Demand Set Aside as Property Used for Residence Despite Lease to Company

articles.title: Markets in Pieces
articles.summary: The article by Disha Joshi and Soham Gupta explores how arbitration and public policy intersect in competition law, emphasizing market coherence over individual correctness. The discussion is framed within India's legal and governance systems, particularly concerning how contractual arrangements influence market behavior.

In competition law, public policy concerns market coherence rather than individual correctness. While arbitration does not regulate markets, it shapes incentives by validating contractual arrangements that affect competitive conditions. articles.summary: The Indian government raised export duties on diesel to ₹55.50 per litre and on aviation turbine fuel (ATF) to ₹42 per litre. articles.title: Anaicut: Predominantly tribal constituency long plagued by neglect of basic infrastructure
articles.summary: Residents of tribal hamlets in Anaicut, a predominantly tribal constituency in the hills, continue to lack basic civic amenities like bitumen roads, reliable water supply, electricity connections, and government hospitals. The neglect has persisted despite the area's tribal majority status, raising concerns about systemic discrimination and unmet welfare needs. The article underscores the urgent need for infrastructure development and policy attention to address these long-standing gaps.
articles.raw_summary: Civic amenities such as bitumen roads, water supply, electricity connection and government hospital still remain a distant dream for residents of many tribal hamlets in the hills

articles.summary: The AIADMK, a political party in Tamil Nadu, has promised a crop loan waiver for farmers and additional loan waivers for persons with disabilities and pavement vendors during the state elections. articles.title: Exclusive: GST on ride-hailing platforms faces fresh scrutiny as industry flags SaaS model mismatch
articles.summary: Industry stakeholders have raised concerns about India's current GST framework for ride-hailing platforms, arguing that the 5% tax on fares collected by e-commerce operators does not adequately differentiate between traditional cab aggregators and SaaS-based platforms. They claim this mismatch creates compliance difficulties, increases costs, and creates regulatory uncertainty. The issue involves ride-hailing companies, industry bodies, and tax authorities.

articles.title: Policy soon for processing of critical minerals Nickel, Lithium to support EVs' value chain
articles.summary: Mines Secretary Piyush Goyal announced that the government is finalizing a policy to support the processing of critical minerals Nickel and Lithium, essential for electric vehicle (EV) manufacturing. The policy will initially focus on these two minerals, with Nickel being particularly important for high-end EVs. This initiative aims to strengthen India's EV value chain and reduce dependency on imports.
articles.raw_summary: Mines Secretary Piyush Goyal said that the proposed policy is at an advanced stage, and will begin with focus on Nickel and Lithium, with both minerals being used within EVs and Nickel being particularly used in high-end EVs.

articles.title: Liquor policy case: Attending RSS event is not grounds for judge recusal, says CBI on Kejriwal plea
articles.summary: The CBI has dismissed allegations of bias against Delhi High Court judge Swarana Kanta Sharma in a liquor policy case, calling the claims 'unscrupulous' and an attempt to undermine the judiciary. The plea by Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal sought the judge's recusal, citing her attendance at an RSS event. The CBI argued that attending such events does not justify recusal, emphasizing the need to uphold judicial authority.
articles.raw_summary: The allegations of bias against judge Swarana Kanta Sharma were ‘unscrupulous’ and amounted to an attempt to lower the authority of the judiciary, CBI said.

articles.title: EXCLUSIVE | West Asia crisis puts FY27 tax assumptions under pressure forcing govt to reassesses estimates
articles.summary: The Indian government is reviewing its tax revenue targets for the fiscal year 2026-27 (FY27) due to rising crude oil prices, the West Asia crisis, and slower economic growth. These factors threaten both direct and indirect tax collections, forcing a reassessment of fiscal assumptions. The government may need to adjust budgetary estimates or policy measures in response. The Centre is evaluating the impact of these external pressures on its revenue projections.
articles.raw_summary: The government is reassessing FY27 tax targets as the West Asia crisis, high crude prices and slower growth threaten both direct and indirect tax collections.

The case highlights systemic failures in handling crimes against children and demands accountability from law enforcement.

The project is expected to have significant implications for water management and infrastructure development in the region.
articles.raw_summary: While the Godavari and the Krishna rivers were already linked in 2015, the current plan will also connect their tributaries as well as some major rivers in northern Andhra Pradesh.

articles.title: What is the SIR controversy in West Bengal | Explained
articles.summary: The article examines the Special Revision of Electoral Rolls (SIR) process in West Bengal, focusing on discrepancies in voter deletions, adjudication of large numbers of voters, and the ECI's role. It explores how these issues have reshaped the state's political landscape and raised questions about electoral fairness. The ECI has identified logical inconsistencies in the process, which has drawn attention to the integrity of voter rolls ahead of elections.
articles.raw_summary: How many names have been deleted from the electoral rolls, and why? What are the logical discrepancies identified by the ECI? Why were large numbers of voters placed under adjudication, and how was this resolved? How has the SIR process reshaped the State’s political contest?

articles.title: Centre plans community kitchens to hold back migrant workers at MSME hubs
articles.summary: The Centre plans to expand community kitchen services to manufacturing clusters (MSME hubs) to retain migrant workers. The initiative may involve setting up new kitchens in collaboration with state governments, industry, and social groups. Existing community kitchens in some states will be extended to these hubs as part of the effort.
articles.raw_summary: The plan is to extend community kitchen services, already in operation in several states, to manufacturing clusters. It may also include setting up new community kitchens with the cooperation of state governments, as well as industry and social groups.

articles.title: West Bengal polls: Didi alleges bid to cancel her nomination in Bhowanipore
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee alleged attempts to cancel her nomination for the Bhowanipore constituency by filing 'false affidavits' by unnamed individuals. Her name remains on the first EVM for the election.

articles.title: DGFT Revises Export Policy for Feathers & Skins in Line with EU/UK Norms
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) has updated India's export policy for feathers and skins to match European Union and United Kingdom standards. The new rules require mandatory veterinary certification for feather exports while maintaining the free export status. This change aims to ensure stricter compliance with international norms. The policy revision is part of India's efforts to align with global trade standards.

The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) is overseeing the transition.

articles.title: Centre's fresh CAFE 3 brew seeks to ease life for car cos
articles.summary: The Centre has introduced new CAFE 3 norms, effective from April 2027, to reduce fuel consumption and CO2 emissions in the automotive sector. Carmakers will earn credits for producing efficient vehicles, EVs, and hybrids, with additional 'super credits' for zero-emission technologies. A three-year compliance block and credit trading system will provide flexibility. By 2031-32, CNG vehicles are expected to dominate the mix under these norms.
articles.raw_summary: New CAFE 3 norms, effective April 2027, aim to slash fuel consumption and CO2 emissions. Carmakers will earn credits for efficient vehicles, EVs, and hybrids, with "super credits" for zero-emission tech. A three-year compliance block and credit trading offer flexibility. CNG vehicles are projected to dominate the mix by 2031-32.

articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) has raised gas supply to fertilizer plants by 5%, reaching around 95% of their six-month average consumption starting April 9, 2026. articles.title: From diversity to monotony, ecological communities are homogenising
articles.summary: The article explores biotic homogenisation, where diverse ecosystems worldwide are losing unique species and becoming increasingly similar due to environmental changes. This process replaces sensitive endemic species with common generalist ones, raising concerns about ecological balance and biodiversity loss. The phenomenon is documented globally but has implications for India's environmental policies and conservation strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Imagine walking through different ecosystems across the world, only to find that species inhabiting them look strikingly similar. This is the reality of biotic homogenisation, a process where diverse biological communities become increasingly similar as unique endemic species, highly sensitive to environmental changes, are replaced by common generalist species, which are insensitive to such perturbations. [ ]

articles.title: Where Relationship Ends In A Breakup, The Man Is In The Lockup: Karnataka HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court quashed criminal proceedings against a man accused of wrongdoing after a relationship ended, ruling that a failed relationship cannot be criminalized without proof of deceit at its inception. The court emphasized that the absence of criminal intent at the start of the relationship invalidates subsequent legal action. This ruling sets a precedent for how relationship disputes are treated under criminal law in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that a failed relationship cannot be criminalised without proof of deceit at inception. It quashed proceedings citing absence of criminal intent. The post Where Relationship Ends In A Breakup, The Man Is In The Lockup: Karnataka HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Recyclable Materials Mean Nothing if the Product Cannot Be Disassembled
articles.summary: Performance outerwear, due to its complex multi-material construction and bonded components, is nearly impossible to recycle. A new research project is testing whether redesigning products for easier disassembly could solve this challenge. The apparel industry's technical complexity has long made end-of-life recovery difficult, but this initiative aims to change that from the design stage. The outcome could inform future policies on sustainable fashion and textile waste management.
articles.raw_summary: Performance outerwear is among the most technically complex categories in apparel—and among the hardest to recycle. Multi-material construction, bonded membranes, and inseparable trims have long made end-of-life recovery a structural impossibility rather than a logistical inconvenience. A new research and development project is testing whether design for disassembly can change that calculus from the ground up.

articles.title: The Sourcing Story: Industry Rewrote Its Geography Without Rewriting Its Dependence
articles.summary: The global garment industry has relocated apparel assembly to new countries over the past decade due to trade tensions and compliance pressures, but key inputs like fabrics and fibres remain concentrated in China. New research shows how production networks have reconfigured without reducing dependence on Chinese industrial inputs. This shift could impact India's role in global supply chains, given its growing manufacturing sector and trade policies. The findings underscore the need for India to
articles.raw_summary: The global garment industry has spent a decade reconfiguring its production geography in response to trade tensions, compliance pressures, and the demands of fast-moving online retail. New research now provides a detailed account of what has changed and what has not. Apparel assembly has moved, measurably and deliberately. The industrial inputs that make that assembly possible—fabrics, fibres, accessories—have stayed in China.

This shift could have significant implications for India's energy security, trade policies, and inflation management as a major importer of crude and metals.

articles.title: Global sea surface temperatures are reaching record highs [Commentary]
articles.summary: Global sea surface temperatures (SST) are reaching record highs, driven by climate change. Oceans absorb most of the planet's excess heat, but the consequences of rising SST—such as marine ecosystem disruptions and extreme weather—are often overshadowed by discussions on air temperatures. This trend underscores the urgent need for climate action, including policy measures to address ocean warming and its impacts.
articles.raw_summary: Rising Sea Surface Temperatures (SST) and their seasonal fluctuations have emerged as among the most visible indicators of a warming planet. Yet, while public discourse on global warming tends to focus on rising air temperatures driven by anthropogenic greenhouse gas emissions, the increase in SST and its profound consequences remain underemphasised. Oceans absorb the vast [ ]

articles.summary: Photographic evidence confirms the presence of Bengal tigers in Dibru Saikhowa National Park and Biosphere Reserve in Assam, India. articles.title: Instagram expands safety settings on teen accounts with 13+ content ratings
articles.summary: Instagram has introduced stricter safety settings for users under 18, defaulting to a 13+ content rating system. These settings cannot be opted out of without parental approval, leading to enhanced content filtering and ranking. The change applies globally, including in India, but is a corporate initiative rather than a government policy action.

Key export hubs include Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Odisha, Lakshadweep, and Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

articles.title: Piyush Goyal holds talks with Saudi Arabia trade minister on stronger supply chains

The discussions also focused on ensuring smooth trade flows and recovering from disruptions.

articles.title: Plastics group seeks 'essential commodity' status amid polymer price shock
articles.summary: The plastics industry body has requested the government to classify plastics as an 'essential commodity' due to a severe cost and supply shock. Raw material prices have surged by 50-70%, while availability is erratic, logistics costs are higher, and LPG shortages affect processing units. The industry seeks policy relief to stabilize prices and ensure supply continuity.
articles.raw_summary: The industry body said the sector is facing a severe cost and supply shock, with raw material prices rising by 50-70%, along with erratic availability, extended lead times, higher logistics costs and shortage of LPG used by several processing units.

articles.title: From queues to QR codes: How UPI transformed India’s digital payments, now driving 49% of global real-time transactions
articles.summary: India's financial landscape has been revolutionized by UPI, the nation's digital payments centerpiece. Launched in 2016, it has become the world's largest real-time payment system by volume, processing billions of transactions monthly. UPI has significantly expanded financial inclusion, bringing millions into the digital economy and setting a global benchmark for inclusive financial innovation.

articles.title: ADB raises India’s FY27 growth forecast to 6.9%
articles.summary: India's economic growth forecast for FY27 has been raised to 6.9 percent by the Asian Development Bank. This upward revision is driven by increasing consumption, higher investment, supportive government policies, and recent trade pacts. While FY26 is projected at 7.6 percent, FY27 anticipates a moderation due to global uncertainties. The outlook for FY28 is a robust 7.

articles.title: J&K LG terminates services of two government employees over terror links
articles.summary: Jammu and Kashmir Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha terminated the services of two government employees for their alleged links with terror outfits, including Lashkar-e-Taiba and Hizbul Mujahideen. articles.title: Himanta wife passport row: Telangana HC gives Pawan Khera 1-week protection, tells him to move Assam court
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court has granted Pawan Khera, a Congress leader, a one-week transit anticipatory bail in a defamation and criminal conspiracy case. The case was filed over Khera's claims about Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife holding multiple passports. The court directed Khera to approach the Assam court for regular anticipatory bail. The incident highlights tensions between political figures and raises questions about defamation laws and free speech.
articles.raw_summary: Defamation, criminal conspiracy case against Khera lodged over his ‘multiple-passport’ claims against Assam CM's wife. HC has allowed transit anticipatory bail.

The crisis stems from geopolitical tensions affecting fuel supply chains, with potential implications for India's energy imports and aviation sector.

articles.title: The three market outcome scenarios modelled by JPMorgan on the US-Iran war
articles.summary: JPMorgan has modeled three potential market outcomes based on a US-Iran war scenario. A bullish outcome would involve de-escalation in West Asia or a US Federal Reserve policy shift, leading to a broad market rally, lower bond yields, falling oil prices, tighter credit spreads, and a weaker US dollar. The scenarios are speculative and depend on geopolitical developments and central bank actions.
articles.raw_summary: A bullish scenario will be when there is proper de-escalation in West Asia or there is a policy pivot by the US Federal Reserve. In such a scenario, they see a broad "everything rally" where equities advance, bond yields drop, oil prices fall, credit spreads tighten and the US Dollar weakens.

articles.summary: A former Israeli government spokesperson stated that the US-Iran ceasefire is fragile because Iran is refusing to allow unhindered passage through the Strait of Hormuz. The Strait is a vital shipping route for global oil supplies, and its closure could disrupt energy markets and trade. The comment highlights ongoing tensions in the region that may impact international relations and energy policies.

articles.title: This time it’s different? Why your brain betrays you in a market crash
articles.summary: Indian markets are near 2023 highs, yet many stocks lag. This psychological concept explains how the 'remembering self' recalls past successes, while the 'experiencing self' feels present fear, leading to the 'this time it's different' fallacy.

articles.title: Jal Jeevan Mission Scam: Retired IAS officer appears in court
articles.summary: A retired IAS officer, Subodh Agarwal, appeared in court in connection with a scam involving the Jal Jeevan Mission, a government scheme providing tap water connections. Agarwal stated he voluntarily filed the FIR and cooperated with the police. The case highlights potential governance and implementation issues in a major public welfare program.
articles.raw_summary: Subodh Agarwal, a Jaipur-based officer said he had volunteered to file the FIR and cooperated with the police.

articles.title: China moves out of factory deflation after 3 years as Iran war impacts producers
articles.summary: China has moved out of factory deflation for the first time in three years after the war in Iran drove up energy costs. Producer prices rose by 0.5% in March, though consumer inflation slowed and manufacturers' profits remained weak. The conflict in Iran has disrupted global energy supplies, which could have broader economic implications for countries like India that rely on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: China exits industrial deflation as war in Iran drives energy costs up, producer prices rise 0.5% in March, but consumer inflation slows and manufacturers profits stay weak

articles.title: MNRE: Use of induction cooktops, day-time charging of EVs will save money for consumers
articles.summary: India's Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) Minister Pralhad Joshi stated that using induction cooktops and charging electric vehicles (EVs) during daytime can reduce power costs for consumers, supported by cheaper solar tariffs. The government is also accelerating domestic production of electric cooking appliances to reduce reliance on imported LPG and LNG amid the West Asia crisis.
articles.raw_summary: Use of induction cooktops and daytime EV charging can significantly lower power costs for consumers, aided by cheaper solar tariffs, said MNRE Minister Pralhad Joshi. The government is also accelerating domestic production of electric cooking appliances to cut reliance on imported LPG and LNG amid the ongoing West Asia crisis.

articles.title: World Bank: South Asian labour market pivoting towards skills that can be amplified by AI
articles.summary: The World Bank's Chief Economist Franziska Ohnsorge noted that hiring has slowed in occupations exposed to AI, with job prospects eroding due to long-standing labour market disparities. This trend is part of a broader shift in South Asian labour markets toward skills that can be enhanced by AI. The findings suggest potential challenges for workforce adaptation and policy interventions in regions like India.
articles.raw_summary: World Bank Group's Chief Economist Franziska Ohnsorge said that hiring has slowed down in most AI-exposed occupations or firms with large productivity gains in that industry, with job prospects in AI-exposed occupations being eroded with persistence of long-standing sub-national labour market differentials.

Sector trends could favour financials and consumption-linked segments, while asset allocation should balance equities, debt and gold amid evolving global conditions.

The situation may prompt further policy support or regulatory action.

articles.title: Too early to draw definitive lessons from West Asia conflict: Navy Chief Admiral Tripathi
articles.summary: Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh Kumar Tripathi stated that it is too early to draw definitive lessons from the West Asia conflict, calling it a critical case study in modern warfare. The remarks were made during a discussion on the evolving nature of conflicts and their potential impact on India's strategic outlook. The statement highlights the need for careful analysis before formulating any policy responses.
articles.raw_summary: Describing the West Asia war as a critical case study in modern warfare, Chief of Naval Staff Admiral Dinesh Kumar Tripathi on Thursday said it was too early to draw definitive lessons from the conflict.

articles.title: SC ends 'matrimonial battle of Mahabharata' after 80 cases, orders husband to pay ₹5-cr alimony
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ended an 80-case matrimonial dispute between a lawyer and his wife, ordering the husband to pay ₹5 crore in alimony. The court criticized the husband for exploiting legal loopholes to prolong the litigation. The ruling underscores systemic issues in family courts and the need for faster justice in gender-related disputes. The case also raises questions about legal ethics and the misuse of legal expertise in personal conflicts.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court criticised the husband, who is a practicing lawyer, for using his legal knowledge to extend the cases unnecessarily.

articles.title: Indians hunger for untested Chinese weight-loss peptides
articles.summary: Influencers and online communities are driving a surge in unapproved weight-loss peptides imported from China. This trend is fueled by the high cost of doctor-prescribed medications and the desire to overcome weight-loss plateaus, as the body can adapt to even potent GLP-1 drugs, leading to weight regain.

articles.title: Explainer: What is in Iran’s 10-point ceasefire plan and will the US agree to it?
articles.summary: The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, with Iran temporarily reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The agreement followed pressure from Donald Trump and involved negotiations with Pakistan and China. Israel also joined the ceasefire. The plan aims to ease tensions in the Middle East, a region critical for global oil supplies.
articles.raw_summary: Two-week ceasefire comes after Trump spoke to Pakistan’s leaders, with China also believed to be exerting influence over Tehran Middle East crisis – live updates US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire with Tehran saying it will reopen strait of Hormuz The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on Tuesday barely an hour before Donald Trump’s deadline to obliterate Iran was set to expire, with Tehran agreeing to temporarily reopen the strait of Hormuz. articles.title: Explainer: India shields consumers from oil shock, but macro risks quietly build beneath the surface
articles.summary: India shields consumers from record energy shock via fuel tax cuts and price controls, but faces rising fiscal strain, external risks, and potential growth and currency pressures

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Why Opec+'s output increase in May won't cool global oil prices
articles.summary: Despite OPEC+'s move to raise output and IEA's emergency release, global crude oil prices are unlikely to fall significantly. The ongoing war in Iran, blocked shipping lanes and attacks on energy infrastructure keep supply tight.
articles.raw_summary: Despite OPEC+'s move to raise output and IEA's emergency release, global crude oil prices are unlikely to fall significantly. The ongoing war in Iran, blocked shipping lanes and attacks on energy infrastructure keep supply tight.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Why India's indigenously developed fast breeder reactor achieving criticality is significant
articles.summary: The indigenously developed Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam reached criticality, a pivotal moment for India's energy future and a significant stride towards fuel autonomy, reducing dependence on uranium imports.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Can AI find bugs humans can’t? Inside Anthropic’s Project Glasswing
articles.summary: Anthropic’s Project Glasswing aims to use advanced AI to detect hard-to-find software vulnerabilities. Backed by Big Tech, the initiative may reshape how software is secured and could eventually disrupt cybersecurity firms and IT services models.
